name: Focaccia Condita
recipeIngredient: 3 green onions, white part trimmed and sliced (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir the yeast into warm water in a mixing bowl and let it proof for 10 minutes.
2. Add the 3 cups of room temperature water and slowly fold the biga into the flour using your fingers. Whisk in the remaining flour and salt, about half a cup at a time, until the dough is no longer able to be whisked.
3. Using your hands, continue mixing until a smooth dough forms. Place on a floured surface and knead for 13 to 15 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
4. Transfer the dough to a large, lightly oiled bowl, cover tightly, and let rise in a cool spot until doubled, about 8 hours.
5. Divide the dough into three pieces. Shape each piece into a rough ball.
6. Oil three 14-inch or 15-inch pizza pans and stretch each dough ball to cover (or roll with a rolling pin). If the dough is resistant, let it rest for a few minutes before continuing.
7. Place the dough on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a silicone mat. Sprinkle generously with cornmeal.
8. Preheat the oven to 450°F.
9. Bake pizzas until the crusts are golden brown, about 20 to 25 minutes. Brush the edges with oil and serve hot.
Variation: Shape the dough into three rounds, place on oiled baking sheets sprinkled with cornmeal, cover, let rise for 2 hours, then bake at 450°F until hollow when tapped. Makes 3 loaves.
